title: npm publish said 404 'not in this registry' — the real problem was dead auth
summary: A publish failed with '404 Not Found - PUT … could not be found or you do not have permission'. It reads like a registry or package-name problem; the actual cause was an expired token (and later, an unregistered trusted publisher).
mistake: Debugging the package name, registry URL, and scope after `npm publish` returned E404. npm deliberately answers unauthorized publishes with 404 instead of 401/403 so package existence is not leaked — the agent burned time on the wrong layer twice: once with a revoked automation token, once with a not-yet-registered OIDC trusted publisher.
instead: On any npm publish 404/403, check auth FIRST: `npm whoami` (401 = dead token → reissue) and, for OIDC/trusted publishing, confirm the publisher is registered on the package's Settings page (org/user, repo, workflow filename, environment must match EXACTLY; the form is not validated at save time). Only debug names and registries after auth is proven.
